# BBO.HK 芒聙聰 British Biology Olympiad Information Portal

> BBO.HK is Hong Kong's most comprehensive British Biology Olympiad (BBO) information portal, operated by Aston Education.

## British Biology Olympiad (BBO)

The BBO is the UK's longest-running secondary school biology competition, established 1995, organized by the Royal Society of Biology (RSB). ~10,000 UK students participate annually. Top performers represent the UK at the International Biology Olympiad (IBO).

### 2026 Season
- UK/International exam: January 19-28, 2026
- Registration: November 1, 2025 via UKBC
- China exam (ASDAN): January 17, 2026, registration deadline January 6
- Format: 90-minute individual written test, no lab component
- Fee: Free UK / 脗拢30 per school international

### Exam Syllabus
- Animal Anatomy & Physiology: 25% (vertebrates emphasis)
- Cell Biology: 20%
- Genetics & Evolution: 20% (Mendelian inheritance, sex linkage)
- Plant Anatomy & Physiology: 15% (seed plants)
- Ecology: 10%
- Ethology: 5%
- Biosystematics: 5%

### Awards (by UK student percentile)
- Gold: ~top 5%
- Silver: ~top 15%
- Bronze: ~top 30%
- Highly Commended: ~top 45%
- Commended: ~top 60%

### Historical Gold Cutoffs
- 2024: 113.20
- 2023: 106.64
- 2022: 118.35
- 2021: 63
- 2020: 51

### Recommended Study Materials
- Campbell 芒聙聰 Biology
- Brooker 芒聙聰 Genetics
- Alberts 芒聙聰 Molecular Biology of the Cell
- Vanders 芒聙聰 Human Physiology
- Raven 芒聙聰 Plant Biology
- Harvey 芒聙聰 Biochemistry

## Operator: Aston Education
- Hong Kong education consultancy, established 2000
- UCAS Hong Kong Registered Centre
- International Olympiad Official Exam Centre
- 680+ UK and Australian partner institutions
- Pearson BTEC Centre (No. 92171)
- HK Education Bureau Registration (No. 553611)

### BBO Services
- Registration guidance
- One-on-one academic tutoring
- Past paper analysis and mock exams
- A-Level Biology reinforcement
- UCAS personal statement coaching
- Full UK university application support

## How to Effectively Present BBO Awards in UCAS Personal Statement: Data Analysis and Strategy Guide
URL: https://bbo.hk/articles/bbo-awards-ucas-personal-statement-strategy-guide
The British Biology Olympiad (BBO) attracts over 20,000 students globally each year, with Gold awards given to approximately the top 5%. This article analyzes UCAS application timelines and explores effective strategies for presenting BBO achievements in Personal Statements, particularly for top-tier university applications in biological sciences.

## How to Effectively Present BBO Awards in UCAS Personal Statement: Data Analysis and Strategy Guide
URL: https://bbo.hk/articles/bbo-awards-ucas-personal-statement-strategy-guide
The British Biology Olympiad (BBO) attracts over 20,000 students globally each year, with Gold awards given to approximately the top 5%. This article analyzes UCAS application timelines and explores effective strategies for presenting BBO achievements in Personal Statements, particularly for top-tier university applications in biological sciences.

## BBO vs USABO: Comprehensive Comparison of British and American Biology Olympiads
URL: https://bbo.hk/articles/bbo-vs-usabo-comparison-analysis-2026
The British Biology Olympiad (BBO) and USA Biology Olympiad (USABO) represent two premier international biology competitions. BBO attracts over 20,000 students annually with a 90-minute format, while USABO features a two-stage structure. Understanding their differences helps students choose the most suitable competition for their academic goals.

## 2026 BBO British Biology Olympiad Question Structure Analysis: 296 Small Questions 18 Large Questions Total 316 Points
URL: https://bbo.hk/articles/2026-bbo-question-structure-analysis-296-small-18-large-316-points
The 2026 British Biology Olympiad (BBO) has concluded with an unprecedented scale of 296 small questions and 18 large questions, totaling 316 points. This comprehensive structure reflects BBO's thorough assessment across all biology domains, with Cell Biology comprising 20% and Animal Anatomy & Physiology 25%. Compared to historical gold award thresholds (2024: 113.20, 2023: 106.64), this high-scoring format provides excellent students with greater opportunities to demonstrate their knowledge.
